pattern: Tracking nesting depth lets you stream through the string and emit only the characters that sit strictly inside each primitive block, dropping the wrapping pair.
hints: A primitive block begins exactly when depth returns to zero and you open a new '('.
Keep a running balance: '(' adds one, ')' subtracts one. The outer pair of every primitive is the '(' that lifts depth from 0 to 1 and the ')' that drops it back to 0.
Append a '(' only when the balance before it is at least 1, and append a ')' only when the balance after it is at least 1 — that skips precisely the outermost pairs.

edge_inputs.description: two flat primitives each reduce to empty
alternating singletons followed by a nested block

stress.description: long random valid parentheses string built by balanced random walk
stress.note: always a valid balanced parentheses string
